# A simple model of global cascades on random networks

**Authors:** Duncan J. Watts  
**Year:** 2002  
**URL:** https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC122850/  
**Evidence type:** Mathematical network model

## Claim supported

Small shocks can trigger rare large cascades in threshold networks under certain structural conditions.

## Key result

The model identifies regimes where networks are susceptible to rare global cascades triggered by small shocks.

## How it supports manufactured traction

This source strengthens the argument that digital systems can be moved by manipulated proxy signals. Use it as evidence for the mechanism, not as proof that every campaign succeeds.
